llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: Selected Experiment 1 (experienced auditors) as the primary comparison. The AI arm uses GPT-4o-powered chatbot. Experienced auditors represent the stronger baseline for evaluating AI assistance on professional fraud risk assessment tasks. Table 4 Panel A provides complete statistics including sample sizes and standard deviations.
llm_extracted.notes: Complete Level A data extracted from Table 4 Panel A (page 47). Sample sizes were found in the table cells: n=38 for No AI Assistance and n=41 for Has Access to AI Assistance. Standard deviations were also provided in the table: sd=2.58 for control and sd=2.84 for AI condition. The p-value is reported as an inequality (p-value= <0.01) rather than an exact value, so reported_p_value is set to null. The outcome is Number of Highly Relevant Risks, where higher values indicate better fraud risk identification performance.
source_quotes: Table 4 Panel A: Experienced - No AI Assistance: mean=3.00, std. dev= 2.58, n= 38
Table 4 Panel A: Experienced - Has Access to AI Assistance: mean=6.39, std. dev= 2.84, n= 41
Number of Highly Relevant Risks is the number of risks identified by each participant that was within a category rated as highly relevant.
